edges.description: MANBA loss directly reduces diagnostic beta-mannosidase activity.
Reduced beta-mannosidase blocks lysosomal breakdown of glycoprotein-derived oligosaccharides.
Stored oligosaccharide substrates overflow into urine as diagnostic biomarkers.
The storage disorder is repeatedly associated with hearing loss, though the exact cochlear intermediate is not resolved.
CNS involvement produces intellectual disability, behavioral disturbance, regression, and occasional seizures.
Patient series show delayed myelination, diffuse hypomyelination, and other MRI abnormalities.
Recurrent skin and respiratory infections are reported in a subset of patients, but the specific immune mechanism is unresolved.
Cutaneous manifestations are reported in some patients, including rare angiokeratomas.
Facial dysmorphism is recorded in the Orphanet beta-mannosidosis phenotype table, but the cached evidence does not resolve the intervening storage mechanism.
Abdominal wall musculature hypoplasia is recorded in the Orphanet beta-mannosidosis phenotype table, with unresolved disease-specific intermediates.
edges.target: Hypoplasia of the abdominal wall musculature

edges.description: Auditory involvement clinically manifests as sensorineural hearing impairment.
CNS involvement is clinically expressed as intellectual disability.
Behavioral disturbance includes hyperactivity, obsessive-compulsive-like behavior, autistic features, and aggression in reported patients.
Developmental regression is part of the newly expanded clinical spectrum.
Dysphagia is part of the newly expanded neurologic and esophageal-motility phenotype spectrum.
Seizures are an occasional neurologic manifestation in severe or early-onset cases.
MRI-defined myelination delay is the phenotype-level expression of this CNS imaging node.
Respiratory infections are the recurrent-infection phenotype supported by the clinical reports.
Cutaneous involvement is reported in some patients, though angiokeratoma is a rare manifestation.
Increased urinary free oligosaccharides report overflow of incompletely degraded glycoprotein-derived substrates from lysosomal storage.
Decreased beta-mannosidase activity directly reports the primary MANBA lysosomal enzyme deficiency.

nodes.description: Brain MRI findings in beta-mannosidosis include delayed myelination, diffuse hypomyelination, white matter hyperintensities, calcification, atrophy, and hydrocephalus, indicating CNS structural involvement beyond isolated cognitive symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Half the patients have abnormal brain MRIs, showing delayed myelination before 2 years of age and diffuse hypomyelination thereafter.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Neuroimaging abnormalities were reported in 40% of published cases and included cortical and subcortical atrophy, basal ganglia and white matter calcification, hydrocephalus, periventricular and subcortical white matter hyperintensities, and delayed myelination.

nodes.description: Dysmorphic or otherwise abnormal facial shape is listed as a very frequent manifestation in the Orphanet beta-mannosidosis phenotype table.

evidence.reference_title: Beta-mannosidosis (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0001999 | Abnormal facial shape | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Cutaneous vascular lesions reported in some cases, with skin cell vacuolation.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: Additional rare clinical signs such as angiokeratomas

nodes.description: Behavioral manifestations include hyperactivity, obsessive-compulsive-like behavior, autistic features, aggression, and other behavioral problems in reported patients.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.

nodes.description: Hearing loss is one of the most prominent and often earliest clinical manifestations in reported beta-mannosidosis cohorts, including the Central European Roma founder-variant series.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: The most prominent and common clinical manifestations are mental retardation reported in ten patients and hearing loss mentioned in 13 patients (Table 2).

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: β-mannosidosis should be considered as a differential diagnosis in patients with syndromic or apparently nonsyndromic hearing loss

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: a decreased activity of the beta-mannosidase enzyme in different cells, particularly in leukocytes

readouts.interpretation: Decreased beta-mannosidase activity directly reports the primary MANBA lysosomal enzyme deficiency.

nodes.description: Cutaneous involvement is reported in some beta-mannosidosis patients, including rare angiokeratomas and recurrent skin infections. The current cached evidence supports the clinical association but not a precise skin-cell storage mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: Additional rare clinical signs such as angiokeratomas

nodes.description: Brain MRI can show delayed myelination in infancy and diffuse hypomyelination later in childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Half the patients have abnormal brain MRIs, showing delayed myelination before 2 years of age and diffuse hypomyelination thereafter.

nodes.description: Developmental regression was newly described in the expanded 2025 patient cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Developmental regression, dysphagia, obsessive-compulsive-like behavior, and erythromelalgia are newly described features associated with β-mannosidosis among the 6 patients from our cohort.

nodes.description: Dysphagia and related swallowing or esophageal motility abnormalities are reported in beta-mannosidosis, including a 2025 cohort and an earlier case with recurrent respiratory infections.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Developmental regression, dysphagia, obsessive-compulsive-like behavior, and erythromelalgia are newly described features associated with β-mannosidosis among the 6 patients from our cohort.

evidence.reference_title: [Beta mannosidosis: a new case].
evidence.snippet: Our patient presented with abnormalities of swallowing and esophageal motility resulting in recurring respiratory infections, previously reported in some other cases.

nodes.description: Impaired lysosomal beta-mannosidase activity leaves glycoprotein-derived oligosaccharides incompletely degraded, with abnormal oligosaccharides detected in patient urine.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: Biochemical diagnostics confirmed the typical disaccharides (primarily Man (beta1 → 4) GlcNac) in the urine of affected patients

evidence.snippet: Oligosaccharides and glycoamino acids have been recognized as biomarkers for MPS and glycoproteinosis.

nodes.description: Biallelic MANBA pathogenic variants reduce lysosomal beta-mannosidase activity, blocking the terminal step of lysosomal glycoprotein degradation.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: β-mannosidosis is an ultra-rare lysosomal storage disorder caused by a deficiency of β-mannosidase, which catalyzes the last step of glycoprotein degradation.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: Beta-mannosidosis is an extremely rare metabolic storage disorder resulting from a beta-mannosidase deficiency that is involved in the degradation of glycoproteins.

nodes.description: Central nervous system involvement is variable but commonly includes intellectual disability and behavioral abnormalities; seizures and developmental regression are less frequent features in the expanded phenotype spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotype Spectrum of beta-Mannosidosis.
evidence.snippet: Developmental regression, dysphagia, obsessive-compulsive-like behavior, and erythromelalgia are newly described features associated with β-mannosidosis among the 6 patients from our cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Variant c.2158-2A>G in MANBA is an important and frequent cause of hereditary hearing loss and beta-mannosidosis among the Czech and Slovak Roma population- evidence for a new ethnic-specific variant.
evidence.snippet: The clinical manifestation varies between mild and severe, involving symptoms such as mental retardation, hyperactivity, behavioural problems, hearing loss, and frequent infections of the skin and respiratory tract.

nodes.description: Recurrent skin and respiratory infections are reported in a subset of beta-mannosidosis patients; one case report links recurrent respiratory infections to aspiration-prone swallowing and esophageal motility abnormalities, while the broader immune mechanism is not established in the cached evidence.
